ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Bibla asaa ya nimatuo alɛ bitookyi ni siikple lee kamɛ.
translated: The Bible is the book that I use to teach the people of the community for their benefit.

ref: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
text: Bita anu lee ate kesekese biɛpɛ ɔlaa bita Yaa alɛ bilaabuo di ɔsɔ onyu kamɛ. Ninwuna komiɛ ɔbla, kafɔɔ suoto sina nnaa osie.”
translated: The story of the people and their small seeds follows the path of Yaa, her journey in the shadowy forest. I hold the knowledge of the elders, with the tools we use every day.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Kafɔɔ nunua yi, kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ bitansinka lɛsalɛsaa kuniwii nse biobuɛ bilɛ bibabla lɛsaa lewo. Bitanka di osi diekye nfa ninle Yaa lɛkakpomii nɛ.
translated: Kafɔɔ nunua yi, kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ bitansinka lɛsalɛsaa kuniwii nse biobuɛ bilɛ bibabla lɛsaa lewo. Bitanka di osi diekye nfa ninle Yaa lɛkakpomii nɛ.

ref: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
text: Obe wɔ baanyɔɔ nii, baatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Otii biala kowolaa fɔ!”
translated: He said to them, "Baatɔkɔ is here, take the biala and go quickly!"

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “Kafɔɔ aye bafũbanutɛ yi, bitanta baalɛɛ ye basaatuotɛ, diekye saatuotɛ onwii lete ninkpe ye. Aye yi, bawo babilɛma bile.
translated: In the time of the bafũbanutɛ, the bitanta and baalɛɛ, the diekye saatuotɛ remains steadfast. This is, the bawo babilɛma.

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Batii lanu sɛlɔ sewo di Rama alɛ boowi kaku osie kanya ku sɛnyaami. Rahel nikowi anta babi nɔɔ. Eesĩ kafɔɔ alɛ batanlo sɔɔ, diekye babi nɔɔ nwu nkpo lekpi.” Yosef, Maria Ku Yesu Lakple Di Egipte Kasɔ Suoto.
translated: "A voice was heard in Ramah, lamentation and bitter weeping. Rachel weeping for her children, refusing to be comforted. They have fled to another land, fleeing with their children by night." Joseph, Mary, and Jesus Flee to Egypt's Journey.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Kafɔɔ kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, bia Ɔka Salomo omu, di lɛsaawɛ nɔɔ nimuu kamɛ, atakpee awu alɛ onyu aawo lepoo kamɛ atoto kpeele ya mmle kaamɛ lɛma onwii.
translated: The wise man said, as King Solomon said, the truth is not easily found; those who seek it must dig deep until they reach the core of understanding.

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Di lɛkankoyi nwu suoto yi, Niniwe batii mataka manyila makanko ye malo ye lɛpɔɔ, diekye baakyi di sikpile lɛma suoto di obe wɔ baanu ni Yona ɔlaa obuɛ. Kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ otii onwii nkpe ninfũ ɛɛtaka katoo alenke ni Yona.
translated: In the days of King Niniwe, Jonah was sent to the city of Nineveh, which was enclosed by walls, to preach repentance to its people. The inhabitants heard the word of the Lord and repented of their evil ways through Jonah.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Kafɔɔ Saate lɛkɔkɔlɛ ayi nwu nkpo. Se diele nkpo ɛɛbla se otii kuonwii dilababu. Kafɔɔ ta batii nɔɔ ba eediki ɛɛta ni suoto oso, ɛɛkɔkɔlɛ ayi nwu.
translated: Kafɔɔ Saate, the guardian of the sacred place, is entrusted with the sacred duty. For the sacred place is known as the source of life and the foundation of communal harmony. Kafɔɔ brings the ancestral wisdom to guide the people along the correct path, the guardian of the sacred duty.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Nle mmle oso, bia miɛ bɔɔlɛɛ kasɔ nwu nkpo alɛ Ntɔ Kasɔ nɛ.
translated: We, the people of the place, come to see the new day's water at Ntɔ Kasɔ.

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: ni aafaa osie kanya aabuɛ alɛ, “Be foomiɛ di ɔkyɛ loo, afɔ Yesu Nasaret Otii ntɛɛ? Ɔwɔɔsa fɛɛwa wo? N-ye otii wɔ fale nii! Afɔ ninle Otii Klekle wɔ di Yaa lekyesee ni nɛ!”
translated: They said to him, the one who knows, “Are you the one who is to come, the prophet of Jesus of Nazareth? Should we just wait? I myself am the one who is coming in truth! The prophet Klekle in the land of Yaa is the one who is coming!”

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: Nioso, ditɔɔwa fɛ mmle bɛɛkpana ni Kɔɔkpana Klekle kamɛ alɛ, ‘Babanya, mankple manya, kafɔɔ blɔbanya batɛnsũ. Babanu, mankple manu, kafɔɔ blɔbanu kasɔ. Nse beenu kasɔ, bɛɛnya bɛɛtɛ nsũ, babakyi siitu lɛma kamɛ, Yaa n-yɔ sikpile lɛma ankyɛ ma!’ ” Yesu Lediki Lɛkpa Le Aapɛ Ni Kasɔ Di Alesaa Kubi Santɛ Suoto Aata Ma.
translated: Nioso, the one who holds the truth of the ancestral lineage and the Klekle covenant, said, 'Father of the living, our hands are bound to the living breath. Father of the departed, our hands are bound to the departed dust. Our souls rest in dust, the living breath and the departed dust are one, the bridge between them is the path of life! Yaa, you who walk the path of life, hold fast to the thread!' Thus spoke Jesus Lediki Lɛkpa, the Keeper of the Covenant for the Alesaa Kubi Santɛ Suoto community.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: “Lɛ diɛbla fɔɔnya diku biibii le ninsi ni di owo lɛfɔ ninu, kafɔɔ flɔɔnya kawosokuu ka ninsi fɔ ni leele kamɛ?
translated: Do the devil's followers take their Bible inside, holding the pages now? But the followers leave without knowing within the forest, will they?
